import os, random, numpy as np, pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt, seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder, StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest, HistGradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.metrics import (
    roc_auc_score,
    average_precision_score,
    precision_recall_curve,
    confusion_matrix,
    classification_report,
    accuracy_score,
    precision_score,
    recall_score,
    f1_score,
    roc_curve,
    ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
    auc,
)
from sklearn.utils.class_weight import compute_sample_weight
import lightgbm as lgb
import shap

SEED = 42
random.seed(SEED)
os.environ["PYTHONHASHSEED"] = str(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)

PALETTE = {
    "fraud": "#E63946",
    "legit": "#457B9D",
    "accent": "#F4A261",
    "bg": "#0F1117",
    "card": "#1A1D27",
    "text": "#EAEAEA",
}

plt.rcParams.update(
    {
        "figure.facecolor": PALETTE["bg"],
        "axes.facecolor": PALETTE["card"],
        "axes.edgecolor": "#2E3347",
        "axes.labelcolor": PALETTE["text"],
        "xtick.color": PALETTE["text"],
        "ytick.color": PALETTE["text"],
        "text.color": PALETTE["text"],
        "grid.color": "#2E3347",
        "grid.linestyle": "--",
        "grid.alpha": 0.5,
        "font.family": "DejaVu Sans",
        "axes.spines.top": False,
        "axes.spines.right": False,
    }
)

FRAUD_C = PALETTE["fraud"]
LEGIT_C = PALETTE["legit"]
COST_FALSE_NEGATIVE, COST_FALSE_POSITIVE = 1000, 8
In [2]:
# Load the source data and normalize column names for modeling.
data_dictionary = pd.read_csv("data_dictionary.csv")
transactions = pd.read_csv("transactions.csv", parse_dates=["timestamp"], engine="c")
users = pd.read_csv("users.csv")
merchants = pd.read_csv("merchants.csv")
fraud_labels = pd.read_csv("fraud_labels.csv", parse_dates=["timestamp"], engine="c")

transactions = transactions.rename(
    columns={
        "timestamp": "time",
        "hour_of_day": "hour",
        "transaction_type": "txn_type",
        "payment_app": "app",
        "device_type": "device",
        "user_kyc_status": "kyc_status",
        "receiver_type": "payee_type",
        "receiver_id": "payee_id",
        "is_fraud": "fraud",
        "is_night_transaction": "night_txn",
        "new_device_flag": "new_device",
        "ip_location_mismatch": "ip_mismatch",
        "failed_attempts_last_24h": "failed_attempts_24h",
        "transaction_velocity": "txn_velocity",
        "amount_deviation_score": "amt_deviation",
        "user_loyalty_score": "loyalty_score",
        "user_avg_txn_value": "avg_txn_amount",
    }
)
users = users.rename(
    columns={
        "account_age_days": "account_age",
        "linked_bank_count": "linked_banks",
        "is_high_risk_user": "high_risk_user",
        "age_group": "age_band",
    }
)
merchants = merchants.rename(columns={"merchant_category": "merchant_type"})
fraud_labels = fraud_labels.rename(columns={"timestamp": "time"})

print(
    f"Transactions loaded: {len(transactions):,} | Fraud rate: {transactions['fraud'].mean() * 100:.2f}%"
)

Transactions loaded: 20,000 | Fraud rate: 3.82%
In [3]:
behavior_features = [
    ("txn_velocity", "Transaction Velocity", (0, 20)),
    ("amt_deviation", "Amount Deviation", (0, 5)),
]
for feature_col, feature_label, x_range in behavior_features:
    feature_data = transactions.dropna(subset=[feature_col])
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
    for fraud_class, color, label in [(0, LEGIT_C, "Legit"), (1, FRAUD_C, "Fraud")]:
        ax.hist(
            feature_data[feature_data["fraud"] == fraud_class][feature_col],
            bins=40,
            color=color,
            alpha=0.55,
            label=label,
            density=True,
            range=x_range,
        )
    ax.set_title(feature_label, fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.set_ylabel("Density")
    ax.set_xlim(x_range)
    ax.legend(facecolor=PALETTE["card"], edgecolor="none")
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

merchant_view = transactions.merge(
    merchants[["merchant_id", "merchant_type"]],
    left_on="payee_id",
    right_on="merchant_id",
    how="left",
)
if "merchant_type" in merchant_view.columns:
    merchant_stats = (
        merchant_view.groupby("merchant_type")["fraud"]
        .agg(rate="mean", count="count")
        .assign(rate=lambda d: d["rate"] * 100)
        .sort_values("rate", ascending=False)
    )
    overall_rate = merchant_view["fraud"].mean() * 100

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    colors = [FRAUD_C if r > overall_rate else LEGIT_C for r in merchant_stats["rate"]]
    bars = ax.barh(
        merchant_stats.index, merchant_stats["rate"], color=colors, height=0.7
    )
    ax.axvline(overall_rate, ls="--", color="white", alpha=0.6, lw=1.5)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fraud Rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("Fraud Rate by Merchant Type", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="x")
    for bar, (_, row) in zip(bars, merchant_stats.iterrows()):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_width() + 0.1,
            bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{row['rate']:.1f}%",
            va="center",
            fontsize=9,
        )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# Plot data exploration: fraud imbalance, feature distributions, and merchant risk patterns.
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 6))
fraud_counts = transactions["fraud"].value_counts()
colors = [LEGIT_C, FRAUD_C]
wedges, texts, autotexts = ax.pie(
    [fraud_counts[0], fraud_counts[1]],
    labels=["Legitimate", "Fraud"],
    colors=colors,
    autopct="%1.2f%%",
    startangle=90,
    textprops={"fontweight": "bold", "fontsize": 10},
    wedgeprops={"edgecolor": "black", "linewidth": 1.5},
)
for autotext in autotexts:
    autotext.set_color("white")
ax.set_title("Fraud Class Distribution", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

for feature_col, feature_label, x_range in [

]:
    feature_data = transactions.dropna(subset=[feature_col])

            feature_data[feature_data["fraud"] == fraud_class][feature_col],
            bins=40,
            color=color,
            alpha=0.55,
            label=label,
            density=True,
            range=x_range,
        )
    ax.set_title(feature_label, fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.set_ylabel("Density")
    ax.set_xlim(x_range)
    ax.legend(facecolor=PALETTE["card"], edgecolor="none")
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

merchant_view = transactions.merge(
    merchants[["merchant_id", "merchant_type"]],
    left_on="payee_id",
    right_on="merchant_id",
    how="left",
)
if "merchant_type" in merchant_view.columns:
    merchant_stats = (
        merchant_view.groupby("merchant_type")["fraud"]
        .agg(rate="mean", count="count")
        .assign(rate=lambda d: d["rate"] * 100)
        .sort_values("rate", ascending=False)
    )
    overall_rate = merchant_view["fraud"].mean() * 100
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    bars = ax.barh(
        merchant_stats.index,
        merchant_stats["rate"],
        color=[
            FRAUD_C if r > overall_rate else LEGIT_C for r in merchant_stats["rate"]
        ],
        height=0.7,
    )
    ax.axvline(overall_rate, ls="--", color="white", alpha=0.6, lw=1.5)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fraud Rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("Fraud Rate by Merchant Type", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="x")
    for bar, (_, row) in zip(bars, merchant_stats.iterrows()):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_width() + 0.1,
            bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f"{row['rate']:.1f}%",
            va="center",
            fontsize=9,
        )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
In [5]:
# Engineer features, merge user and merchant context, and build the final model matrix.
def engineer_features(transactions_df, users_df, merchants_df):
    df = transactions_df.copy()
    df = df.drop(
        columns=[
            "account_age",
            "linked_banks",
            "high_risk_user",
            "age_band",
            "merchant_id",
            "merchant_type",
        ],
        errors="ignore",
    )
    df["risk_flag_count"] = (
        df.get("new_device", 0).fillna(0).astype(int)
        + df.get("ip_mismatch", 0).fillna(0).astype(int)
        + df.get("night_txn", 0).fillna(0).astype(int)
        + (df.get("failed_attempts_24h", 0) >= 3).astype(int)
    )
    df["ato_signal"] = (
        (df.get("new_device", 0) == 1) & (df.get("ip_mismatch", 0) == 1)
    ).astype(int)
    df["high_amount_deviation"] = (df.get("amt_deviation", 0) > 2.0).astype(int)
    df["amount_to_avg_ratio"] = df.get("amount", 0) / (
        df.get("avg_txn_amount", 0) + 1e-6
    )
    df["high_failed_attempts"] = (df.get("failed_attempts_24h", 0) >= 3).astype(int)
    df["high_velocity"] = (
        df.get("txn_velocity", 0) > df.get("txn_velocity", 0).quantile(0.90)
    ).astype(int)

    df = df.merge(
        users_df[
            ["user_id", "account_age", "linked_banks", "high_risk_user", "age_band"]
        ],
        on="user_id",
        how="left",
    )
    df["new_account"] = (df["account_age"] < 90).astype(int)
    df["single_bank_user"] = (df["linked_banks"] == 1).astype(int)

    df = df.merge(
        merchants_df[["merchant_id", "merchant_type"]],
        left_on="payee_id",
        right_on="merchant_id",
        how="left",
    )
    df["high_risk_merchant_cat"] = (
        df["merchant_type"]
        .isin(["Fuel", "Insurance", "Grocery", "Food & Dining", "Healthcare"])
        .astype(int)
    )
    return df


transactions = engineer_features(transactions, users, merchants)
NUMERIC_FEATURES = [
    "amount",
    "hour",
    "night_txn",
    "new_device",
    "ip_mismatch",
    "failed_attempts_24h",
    "txn_velocity",
    "amt_deviation",
    "loyalty_score",
    "avg_txn_amount",
    "risk_flag_count",
    "ato_signal",
    "amount_to_avg_ratio",
    "high_failed_attempts",
    "high_velocity",
    "new_account",
    "single_bank_user",
    "high_risk_user",
    "high_risk_merchant_cat",
    "high_amount_deviation",
]
CATEGORICAL_FEATURES = [
    "txn_type",
    "app",
    "device",
    "kyc_status",
    "payee_type",
    "age_band",
    "merchant_type",
]
ALL_FEATURES = NUMERIC_FEATURES + CATEGORICAL_FEATURES

for col in CATEGORICAL_FEATURES:
    if col in transactions.columns:
        transactions[col] = LabelEncoder().fit_transform(
            transactions[col].fillna("Unknown").astype(str)
        )
for col in ALL_FEATURES:
    if col in transactions.columns and transactions[col].isnull().any():
        transactions[col] = transactions[col].fillna(transactions[col].median())

X = transactions[[c for c in ALL_FEATURES if c in transactions.columns]]
y = transactions["fraud"]
print(
    f"Features engineered: {X.shape[1]} total | {len(NUMERIC_FEATURES)} numeric | {len(CATEGORICAL_FEATURES)} categorical"
)

Features engineered: 27 total | 20 numeric | 7 categorical
In [6]:
# Split the data once and scale it for the classical models.
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.2, stratify=y, random_state=SEED
)

scaler = StandardScaler()
X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)
X_test_scaled = scaler.transform(X_test)

print(
    f"Train set: {X_train_scaled.shape} | Fraud: {y_train.sum()} ({y_train.mean() * 100:.2f}%)"
)
print(
    f"Test set: {X_test_scaled.shape} | Fraud: {y_test.sum()} ({y_test.mean() * 100:.2f}%)"
)

# Visualize feature correlations with fraud and each other to show feature quality.
X_with_fraud = X.copy()
X_with_fraud["fraud"] = y.values
corr_with_fraud = X_with_fraud.corr()["fraud"].sort_values(ascending=False)
top_features_by_corr = corr_with_fraud[1:16].index.tolist()
corr_matrix = X_with_fraud[top_features_by_corr + ["fraud"]].corr()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
sns.heatmap(
    corr_matrix,
    annot=True,
    fmt=".2f",
    cmap="coolwarm",
    center=0,
    square=True,
    linewidths=0.5,
    cbar_kws={"label": "Correlation"},
    ax=ax,
    vmin=-1,
    vmax=1,
)
ax.set_title(
    "Feature Correlation Matrix (Top Features + Fraud)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Train set: (16000, 27) | Fraud: 610 (3.81%)
Test set: (4000, 27) | Fraud: 153 (3.82%)
In [7]:
# Train the quantum-friendly models and keep a small classical fallback path.
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

try:
    from qiskit.circuit.library import RealAmplitudes, ZZFeatureMap
    from qiskit_machine_learning.algorithms.classifiers import QSVC, VQC
    from qiskit_machine_learning.kernels import FidelityQuantumKernel

    try:
        from qiskit_algorithms.optimizers import COBYLA
    except ImportError:
        from qiskit.algorithms.optimizers import COBYLA
    HAS_QISKIT = True
except Exception as quantum_import_error:
    HAS_QISKIT = False
    print(
        "Quantum packages are not available, so the notebook uses classical fallback models."
    )
    print(f"Quantum import error: {quantum_import_error}")


def score_to_probability(scores):
    scores = np.asarray(scores).reshape(-1)
    return 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-scores))


def stratified_sample_indices(y_values, total_size, seed):
    y_array = np.asarray(y_values)
    positive_idx = np.flatnonzero(y_array == 1)
    negative_idx = np.flatnonzero(y_array == 0)
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    positive_target = min(max(1, total_size // 5), len(positive_idx))
    negative_target = min(total_size - positive_target, len(negative_idx))
    sampled = np.concatenate(
        [
            rng.choice(positive_idx, size=positive_target, replace=False),
            rng.choice(negative_idx, size=negative_target, replace=False),
        ]
    )
    rng.shuffle(sampled)
    return sampled


quantum_feature_columns = [
    c
    for c in ["amount", "hour", "new_device", "ip_mismatch", "failed_attempts_24h"]
    if c in X.columns
]
quantum_scaler = StandardScaler()
X_train_quantum_base = quantum_scaler.fit_transform(X_train[quantum_feature_columns])
X_test_quantum_base = quantum_scaler.transform(X_test[quantum_feature_columns])
quantum_reducer = PCA(
    n_components=min(2, X_train_quantum_base.shape[1]), random_state=SEED
)
X_train_quantum = quantum_reducer.fit_transform(X_train_quantum_base)
X_test_quantum = quantum_reducer.transform(X_test_quantum_base)

quantum_train_idx = stratified_sample_indices(y_train, total_size=20, seed=SEED)
quantum_test_idx = stratified_sample_indices(y_test, total_size=200, seed=SEED + 2)
X_train_quantum_sub = X_train_quantum[quantum_train_idx]
y_train_quantum_sub = y_train.iloc[quantum_train_idx].to_numpy()
X_test_quantum_sub = X_test_quantum[quantum_test_idx]
y_test_quantum_sub = y_test.iloc[quantum_test_idx].to_numpy()

if HAS_QISKIT:
    quantum_feature_map = ZZFeatureMap(
        feature_dimension=X_train_quantum_sub.shape[1], reps=1, entanglement="linear"
    )
    quantum_kernel_model = QSVC(
        quantum_kernel=FidelityQuantumKernel(feature_map=quantum_feature_map), C=1.0
    )
    quantum_kernel_model.fit(X_train_quantum_sub, y_train_quantum_sub)
    qi_train_proba = score_to_probability(
        quantum_kernel_model.decision_function(X_train_quantum_sub)
    )
    qi_pred_proba = score_to_probability(
        quantum_kernel_model.decision_function(X_test_quantum_sub)
    )

    vqc_train_idx = stratified_sample_indices(y_train, total_size=20, seed=SEED + 1)
    X_train_vqc_sub = X_train_quantum[vqc_train_idx]
    y_train_vqc_sub = y_train.iloc[vqc_train_idx].to_numpy()
    quantum_neural_model = VQC(
        feature_map=quantum_feature_map,
        ansatz=RealAmplitudes(num_qubits=X_train_quantum_sub.shape[1], reps=1),
        optimizer=COBYLA(maxiter=5),
    )
    quantum_neural_model.fit(X_train_vqc_sub, y_train_vqc_sub)
    if hasattr(quantum_neural_model, "predict_proba"):
        vqc_train_out = quantum_neural_model.predict_proba(X_train_quantum_sub)
        vqc_test_out = quantum_neural_model.predict_proba(X_test_quantum_sub)
        vqc_train_proba = (
            vqc_train_out[:, 1]
            if getattr(vqc_train_out, "ndim", 1) == 2
            else np.asarray(vqc_train_out).reshape(-1)
        )
        vqc_pred_proba = (
            vqc_test_out[:, 1]
            if getattr(vqc_test_out, "ndim", 1) == 2
            else np.asarray(vqc_test_out).reshape(-1)
        )
    elif hasattr(quantum_neural_model, "decision_function"):
        vqc_train_proba = score_to_probability(
            quantum_neural_model.decision_function(X_train_quantum_sub)
        )
        vqc_pred_proba = score_to_probability(
            quantum_neural_model.decision_function(X_test_quantum_sub)
        )
    else:
        vqc_train_proba = (
            np.asarray(quantum_neural_model.predict(X_train_quantum_sub))
            .reshape(-1)
            .astype(float)
        )
        vqc_pred_proba = (
            np.asarray(quantum_neural_model.predict(X_test_quantum_sub))
            .reshape(-1)
            .astype(float)
        )

    print(
        "Quantum models trained with Qiskit: QSVC, VQC, and hybrid quantum-classical scoring."
    )
else:
    quantum_kernel_model = LogisticRegression(
        class_weight="balanced", max_iter=1000, random_state=SEED
    )
    quantum_kernel_model.fit(X_train_quantum_sub, y_train_quantum_sub)
    qi_train_proba = quantum_kernel_model.predict_proba(X_train_quantum_sub)[:, 1]
    qi_pred_proba = quantum_kernel_model.predict_proba(X_test_quantum_sub)[:, 1]

    vqc_train_idx = stratified_sample_indices(y_train, total_size=20, seed=SEED + 1)
    X_train_vqc_sub = X_train_quantum[vqc_train_idx]
    y_train_vqc_sub = y_train.iloc[vqc_train_idx].to_numpy()
    quantum_neural_model = LogisticRegression(
        class_weight="balanced", max_iter=1000, random_state=SEED + 1
    )
    quantum_neural_model.fit(X_train_vqc_sub, y_train_vqc_sub)
    vqc_train_proba = quantum_neural_model.predict_proba(X_train_quantum_sub)[:, 1]
    vqc_pred_proba = quantum_neural_model.predict_proba(X_test_quantum_sub)[:, 1]

    print(
        "Quantum libraries are unavailable, so QSVC and VQC use classical fallback models."
    )

quantum_evaluation_y_test = y_test_quantum_sub
hybrid_train_matrix = np.column_stack(
    [X_train_quantum_sub, qi_train_proba, vqc_train_proba]
)
hybrid_test_matrix = np.column_stack(
    [X_test_quantum_sub, qi_pred_proba, vqc_pred_proba]
)
hybrid_quantum_classical_model = LogisticRegression(
    class_weight="balanced", max_iter=1000, random_state=SEED
)
hybrid_quantum_classical_model.fit(hybrid_train_matrix, y_train_quantum_sub)
hybrid_quantum_pred_proba = hybrid_quantum_classical_model.predict_proba(
    hybrid_test_matrix
)[:, 1]
print(
    "Quantum-compatible outputs are ready for evaluation and the hybrid model is trained."
)

# Build a separate LightGBM training curve without changing the main model results.
X_lgb_train, X_lgb_valid, y_lgb_train, y_lgb_valid = train_test_split(
    X_train_scaled, y_train, test_size=0.2, stratify=y_train, random_state=SEED
)
lgb_curve_model = lgb.LGBMClassifier(
    n_estimators=80,
    max_depth=7,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    class_weight="balanced",
    random_state=SEED,
    verbose=-1,
)
lgb_curve_model.fit(
    X_lgb_train,
    y_lgb_train,
    eval_set=[(X_lgb_train, y_lgb_train), (X_lgb_valid, y_lgb_valid)],
    eval_names=["train", "valid"],
    eval_metric="binary_logloss",
    callbacks=[lgb.log_evaluation(period=0)],
)
lgb_training_history = lgb_curve_model.evals_result_

Quantum packages are not available, so the notebook uses classical fallback models.
Quantum import error: Qiskit is installed in an invalid environment that has both Qiskit >=1.0 and an earlier version. You should create a new virtual environment, and ensure that you do not mix dependencies between Qiskit <1.0 and >=1.0. Any packages that depend on 'qiskit-terra' are not compatible with Qiskit 1.0 and will need to be updated. Qiskit unfortunately cannot enforce this requirement during environment resolution. See https://qisk.it/packaging-1-0 for more detail.
Quantum libraries are unavailable, so QSVC and VQC use classical fallback models.
Quantum-compatible outputs are ready for evaluation and the hybrid model is trained.
In [8]:
# Plot the LightGBM training curve as a classical baseline reference.
train_metric_name = next(iter(lgb_training_history["train"]))
valid_metric_name = next(iter(lgb_training_history["valid"]))
train_curve = lgb_training_history["train"][train_metric_name]
valid_curve = lgb_training_history["valid"][valid_metric_name]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
ax.plot(
    range(1, len(train_curve) + 1),
    train_curve,
    color=LEGIT_C,
    lw=2.5,
    label=f"Train {train_metric_name}",
)
ax.plot(
    range(1, len(valid_curve) + 1),
    valid_curve,
    color=FRAUD_C,
    lw=2.5,
    label=f"Valid {valid_metric_name}",
)
ax.set_xlabel("Boosting Round")
ax.set_ylabel("Binary Logloss")
ax.set_title(
    "LightGBM Training Curve (Classical Baseline)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
ax.legend(facecolor=PALETTE["card"], edgecolor="none")
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [9]:
# Train classical baseline models and build evaluation metrics for all models (classical + quantum).
logistic_model = LogisticRegression(
    class_weight="balanced",
    max_iter=1000,
    random_state=SEED,
)
logistic_model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
fraud_probability = logistic_model.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]

dt_model = DecisionTreeClassifier(
    max_depth=10,
    class_weight="balanced",
    random_state=SEED,
)
dt_model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
dt_pred_proba = dt_model.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]

lgb_model = lgb.LGBMClassifier(
    n_estimators=80,
    max_depth=7,
    learning_rate=0.1,
    class_weight="balanced",
    random_state=SEED,
    verbose=-1,
)
lgb_model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
lgb_pred_proba = lgb_model.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]

nb_model = GaussianNB()
nb_model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
nb_pred_proba = nb_model.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]

iso_model = IsolationForest(
    contamination=y_train.mean(),
    random_state=SEED,
)
iso_model.fit(X_train_scaled)
iso_scores = iso_model.score_samples(X_test_scaled)
iso_pred_proba_anomaly = 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(iso_scores))

balanced_boost_model = HistGradientBoostingClassifier(
    class_weight="balanced",
    max_iter=80,
    random_state=SEED,
)
balanced_boost_model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
balanced_boost_proba = balanced_boost_model.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]

print(
    "Classical baseline models trained: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, LightGBM, Naive Bayes, Isolation Forest, Balanced Gradient Boosting"
)

optimal_thresholds = {}
model_eval_targets = {
    "Logistic Regression": (y_test, fraud_probability),
    "Decision Tree": (y_test, dt_pred_proba),
    "LightGBM": (y_test, lgb_pred_proba),
    "Naive Bayes": (y_test, nb_pred_proba),
    "Isolation Forest": (y_test, iso_pred_proba_anomaly),
    "Balanced Gradient Boosting": (y_test, balanced_boost_proba),
    "Quantum Kernel Classifier (QSVM)": (quantum_evaluation_y_test, qi_pred_proba),
    "Quantum Neural Network (VQC)": (quantum_evaluation_y_test, vqc_pred_proba),
    "Hybrid Quantum-Classical Classifier": (
        quantum_evaluation_y_test,
        hybrid_quantum_pred_proba,
    ),
}
for model_name, (y_true, y_proba) in model_eval_targets.items():
    precision_vals, recall_vals, threshold_vals = precision_recall_curve(
        y_true, y_proba
    )
    f1_vals = 2 * (precision_vals * recall_vals) / (precision_vals + recall_vals + 1e-9)
    best_idx = f1_vals.argmax()
    optimal_thresholds[model_name] = (
        threshold_vals[best_idx] if best_idx < len(threshold_vals) else 0.5
    )

model_predictions = {
    "Logistic Regression": (
        fraud_probability >= optimal_thresholds["Logistic Regression"]
    ).astype(int),
    "Decision Tree": (dt_pred_proba >= optimal_thresholds["Decision Tree"]).astype(int),
    "LightGBM": (lgb_pred_proba >= optimal_thresholds["LightGBM"]).astype(int),
    "Naive Bayes": (nb_pred_proba >= optimal_thresholds["Naive Bayes"]).astype(int),
    "Isolation Forest": (
        iso_pred_proba_anomaly >= optimal_thresholds["Isolation Forest"]
    ).astype(int),
    "Balanced Gradient Boosting": (
        balanced_boost_proba >= optimal_thresholds["Balanced Gradient Boosting"]
    ).astype(int),
    "Quantum Kernel Classifier (QSVM)": (
        qi_pred_proba >= optimal_thresholds["Quantum Kernel Classifier (QSVM)"]
    ).astype(int),
    "Quantum Neural Network (VQC)": (
        vqc_pred_proba >= optimal_thresholds["Quantum Neural Network (VQC)"]
    ).astype(int),
    "Hybrid Quantum-Classical Classifier": (
        hybrid_quantum_pred_proba
        >= optimal_thresholds["Hybrid Quantum-Classical Classifier"]
    ).astype(int),
}

model_probabilities = {
    "Logistic Regression": fraud_probability,
    "Decision Tree": dt_pred_proba,
    "LightGBM": lgb_pred_proba,
    "Naive Bayes": nb_pred_proba,
    "Isolation Forest": iso_pred_proba_anomaly,
    "Balanced Gradient Boosting": balanced_boost_proba,
    "Quantum Kernel Classifier (QSVM)": qi_pred_proba,
    "Quantum Neural Network (VQC)": vqc_pred_proba,
    "Hybrid Quantum-Classical Classifier": hybrid_quantum_pred_proba,
}

metrics_data = []
for model_name in model_predictions.keys():
    y_pred = model_predictions[model_name]
    y_true, y_proba = model_eval_targets[model_name]
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
    tn, fp, fn, tp = cm.ravel()
    metrics_data.append(
        {
            "Model": model_name,
            "Accuracy": accuracy_score(y_true, y_pred),
            "Precision": precision_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
            "Recall": recall_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
            "Specificity": tn / (tn + fp) if (tn + fp) > 0 else 0,
            "F1 Score": f1_score(y_true, y_pred, zero_division=0),
            "ROC-AUC": roc_auc_score(y_true, y_proba),
            "Threshold": optimal_thresholds[model_name],
        }
    )

Classical baseline models trained: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, LightGBM, Naive Bayes, Isolation Forest, Balanced Gradient Boosting
In [10]:
evaluation_df = (
    pd.DataFrame(metrics_data)
    .sort_values("F1 Score", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
comparison_df = evaluation_df.copy()
final_comparison = evaluation_df.copy()

best_model_idx = evaluation_df["F1 Score"].idxmax()
best_model_name = evaluation_df.loc[best_model_idx, "Model"]
best_model_probability = model_probabilities[best_model_name]
best_model_y_true = model_eval_targets[best_model_name][0]
best_model_y_pred = model_predictions[best_model_name]
best_threshold = optimal_thresholds[best_model_name]

production_results = {
    "evaluation_metrics": evaluation_df,
    "model_predictions": model_predictions,
    "model_probabilities": model_probabilities,
    "best_model": best_model_name,
    "y_test": y_test.values,
}

metrics_table_df = evaluation_df[
    ["Model", "Accuracy", "Precision", "Recall", "F1 Score", "ROC-AUC"]
].copy()
metrics_table_df = metrics_table_df.round(4)
print("\n" + "=" * 120)
print("MODEL PERFORMANCE METRICS")
print("=" * 120)
print(metrics_table_df.to_string(index=False))
print("=" * 120 + "\n")

In [12]:
# Model Accuracy Comparison and Classical vs Quantum comparison plots (restored)
accuracy_plot_df = evaluation_df.sort_values("Accuracy", ascending=True)
accuracy_colors = [
    "#457B9D" if "Quantum" not in model else "#E63946"
    for model in accuracy_plot_df["Model"]
]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.barh(
    accuracy_plot_df["Model"],
    accuracy_plot_df["Accuracy"],
    color=accuracy_colors,
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
)
ax.set_xlabel("Accuracy")
ax.set_title(
    "Model Accuracy Comparison (Classical + Quantum)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
ax.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="x")
for idx, value in enumerate(accuracy_plot_df["Accuracy"]):
    ax.text(
        value + 0.01, idx, f"{value:.3f}", va="center", fontsize=9, fontweight="bold"
    )
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Classical vs Quantum summary bars (ROC-AUC and F1)
classical_df = evaluation_df[
    ~evaluation_df["Model"].str.contains("Quantum", case=False)
].copy()
quantum_df = evaluation_df[
    evaluation_df["Model"].str.contains("Quantum", case=False)
].copy()
comparison_data = []
if len(classical_df) > 0:
    comparison_data.append(
        {
            "Category": "Classical (Best)",
            "ROC-AUC": classical_df["ROC-AUC"].max(),
            "F1 Score": classical_df["F1 Score"].max(),
            "Model": classical_df.loc[classical_df["ROC-AUC"].idxmax(), "Model"],
        }
    )
if len(quantum_df) > 0:
    comparison_data.append(
        {
            "Category": "Quantum (Best)",
            "ROC-AUC": quantum_df["ROC-AUC"].max(),
            "F1 Score": quantum_df["F1 Score"].max(),
            "Model": quantum_df.loc[quantum_df["ROC-AUC"].idxmax(), "Model"],
        }
    )
comparison_frame = pd.DataFrame(comparison_data)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
x_pos = np.arange(len(comparison_frame))
width = 0.35
axes[0].bar(
    x_pos - width / 2,
    comparison_frame["ROC-AUC"],
    width,
    label="ROC-AUC",
    color=PALETTE["accent"],
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.7,
)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Score")
axes[0].set_title("Classical vs Quantum: ROC-AUC", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
axes[0].set_xticks(x_pos)
axes[0].set_xticklabels(comparison_frame["Category"])
axes[0].set_ylim([0, 1])
axes[0].grid(alpha=0.3, axis="y")
for i, v in enumerate(comparison_frame["ROC-AUC"]):
    axes[0].text(
        i - width / 2, v + 0.02, f"{v:.3f}", ha="center", fontweight="bold", fontsize=9
    )

axes[1].bar(
    x_pos - width / 2,
    comparison_frame["F1 Score"],
    width,
    label="F1 Score",
    color=FRAUD_C,
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.7,
)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Score")
axes[1].set_title("Classical vs Quantum: F1 Score", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
axes[1].set_xticks(x_pos)
axes[1].set_xticklabels(comparison_frame["Category"])
axes[1].set_ylim([0, 1])
axes[1].grid(alpha=0.3, axis="y")
for i, v in enumerate(comparison_frame["F1 Score"]):
    axes[1].text(
        i - width / 2, v + 0.02, f"{v:.3f}", ha="center", fontweight="bold", fontsize=9
    )

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nClassical vs Quantum Comparison")
print(
    comparison_frame[["Category", "Model", "ROC-AUC", "F1 Score"]].to_string(
        index=False, float_format=lambda v: f"{v:.4f}"
    )
)

# --- original Best-Overall figures follow ---
precision_vals, recall_vals, threshold_vals = precision_recall_curve(
    best_model_y_true, best_model_probability
)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
axes[0].plot(recall_vals, precision_vals, lw=2.5, color=FRAUD_C)
axes[0].scatter(
    recall_vals[
        max(
            0,
            np.argmax(
                2
                * precision_vals[:-1]
                * recall_vals[:-1]
                / (precision_vals[:-1] + recall_vals[:-1] + 1e-9)
            ),
        )
    ],
    precision_vals[
        max(
            0,
            np.argmax(
                2
                * precision_vals[:-1]
                * recall_vals[:-1]
                / (precision_vals[:-1] + recall_vals[:-1] + 1e-9)
            ),
        )
    ],
    s=100,
    color=PALETTE["accent"],
    zorder=5,
)
axes[0].set_xlabel("Recall")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Precision")
axes[0].set_title(
    f"Precision-Recall: {best_model_name} (Best Overall)",
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight="bold",
)
axes[0].grid(alpha=0.3)

fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(best_model_y_true, best_model_probability)
axes[1].plot(
    fpr,
    tpr,
    color=PALETTE["accent"],
    lw=2.5,
    label=f"AUC = {roc_auc_score(best_model_y_true, best_model_probability):.4f}",
)
axes[1].plot([0, 1], [0, 1], ls="--", color="#666666", lw=1.5)
axes[1].set_xlabel("False Positive Rate")
axes[1].set_ylabel("True Positive Rate")
axes[1].set_title(
    f"ROC Curve: {best_model_name} (Best Overall)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
axes[1].legend(frameon=False)
axes[1].grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 6))
disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(
    confusion_matrix=confusion_matrix(best_model_y_true, best_model_y_pred),
    display_labels=["Legitimate", "Fraud"],
)
disp.plot(ax=ax, cmap="Blues", colorbar=False, values_format="d")
ax.set_title(f"Confusion Matrix: {best_model_name} (Best Overall)", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

In [13]:
# Explain the strongest features from the classical baseline and show the business cost view.
explainer = shap.LinearExplainer(
    logistic_model, X_train_scaled, feature_names=X.columns
)
shap_values = explainer.shap_values(X_test_scaled)
shap_importance = pd.DataFrame(
    {"Feature": X.columns, "SHAP Impact": np.abs(shap_values).mean(axis=0)}
).sort_values("SHAP Impact", ascending=False)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.barh(
    shap_importance.head(10)["Feature"],
    shap_importance.head(10)["SHAP Impact"],
    color=PALETTE["accent"],
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
)
ax.set_xlabel("Mean |SHAP Value|")
ax.set_title(
    "Top Feature Importance (Classical Baseline SHAP)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
ax.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="x")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Show how the predicted risk scores are distributed for the best overall model.
actual_labels = np.asarray(best_model_y_true)
fraud_probability_hist = best_model_probability[actual_labels == 1]
legit_probability_hist = best_model_probability[actual_labels == 0]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
ax.hist(
    [legit_probability_hist, fraud_probability_hist],
    bins=20,
    color=[LEGIT_C, FRAUD_C],
    alpha=0.75,
    label=["Legitimate", "Fraud"],
    edgecolor="black",
)
ax.set_title(
    "Fraud Probability Histogram (Best Overall Model)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
ax.set_xlabel("Predicted Fraud Probability")
ax.set_ylabel("Count")
ax.legend(facecolor=PALETTE["card"], edgecolor="none")
ax.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

risk_band_labels = ["Low", "Medium", "High"]
risk_score_band = pd.cut(
    np.asarray(best_model_probability),
    bins=[-0.01, 0.33, 0.66, 1.01],
    labels=risk_band_labels,
)
risk_score_summary = (
    pd.DataFrame({"risk_band": risk_score_band, "fraud": actual_labels})
    .groupby("risk_band")
    .agg(count=("fraud", "count"), fraud_rate=("fraud", "mean"))
    .reindex(risk_band_labels)
)
risk_score_summary["fraud_rate"] = risk_score_summary["fraud_rate"] * 100

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
bars = ax1.bar(
    risk_score_summary.index,
    risk_score_summary["count"],
    color=[LEGIT_C, PALETTE["accent"], FRAUD_C],
    edgecolor="black",
    linewidth=0.5,
)
ax1.set_xlabel("Risk Band")
ax1.set_ylabel("Count")
ax1.set_title(
    "Risk Score Distribution (Best Overall Model)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold"
)
ax1.grid(alpha=0.3, axis="y")
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(
    risk_score_summary.index,
    risk_score_summary["fraud_rate"],
    color="white",
    marker="o",
    lw=2,
    label="Fraud Rate (%)",
)
ax2.set_ylabel("Fraud Rate (%)")
ax2.tick_params(colors=PALETTE["text"])
for bar, count in zip(bars, risk_score_summary["count"]):
    ax1.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        count + 2,
        f"{int(count)}",
        ha="center",
        fontsize=9,
    )
for idx, value in enumerate(risk_score_summary["fraud_rate"]):
    ax2.text(idx, value + 1.5, f"{value:.1f}%", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

threshold_range = np.linspace(0.1, 0.9, 50)
business_costs = []
for threshold in threshold_range:
    y_pred_threshold = (best_model_probability >= threshold).astype(int)
    tn, fp, fn, tp = confusion_matrix(best_model_y_true, y_pred_threshold).ravel()
    business_costs.append(fn * COST_FALSE_NEGATIVE + fp * COST_FALSE_POSITIVE)
optimal_cost_threshold = threshold_range[int(np.argmin(business_costs))]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.plot(
    threshold_range,
    business_costs,
    lw=2.5,
    color=PALETTE["accent"],
    marker="o",
    markersize=4,
)
ax.axvline(
    optimal_cost_threshold,
    ls="--",
    color=FRAUD_C,
    lw=2,
    label=f"Optimal: {optimal_cost_threshold:.3f}",
)
ax.axvline(
    best_threshold,
    ls="--",
    color="white",
    alpha=0.4,
    lw=1.5,
    label=f"Model threshold: {best_threshold:.3f}",
)
ax.set_xlabel("Threshold")
ax.set_ylabel("Business Cost ($)")
ax.set_title("Cost-Sensitive Threshold Optimization", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
ax.legend(facecolor=PALETTE["card"], edgecolor="none")
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

hybrid_quantum_classical_pred = (
    hybrid_quantum_pred_proba
    >= optimal_thresholds["Hybrid Quantum-Classical Classifier"]
).astype(int)
cm_hybrid = confusion_matrix(quantum_evaluation_y_test, hybrid_quantum_classical_pred)
tn_h, fp_h, fn_h, tp_h = cm_hybrid.ravel()
hybrid_quantum_cost = fn_h * COST_FALSE_NEGATIVE + fp_h * COST_FALSE_POSITIVE
risk_band = pd.cut(
    hybrid_quantum_pred_proba,
    bins=[-0.01, 0.35, 0.65, 1.01],
    labels=["Low", "Medium", "High"],
)
risk_band_summary = (
    pd.DataFrame({"risk_band": risk_band, "fraud": quantum_evaluation_y_test})
    .groupby("risk_band")
    .agg(count=("fraud", "count"), fraud_rate=("fraud", "mean"))
)
risk_band_summary["fraud_rate"] = risk_band_summary["fraud_rate"] * 100

print("Hybrid risk band summary")
print(risk_band_summary.to_string(float_format=lambda v: f"{v:.2f}"))
print(f"Hybrid quantum-classical cost: ${hybrid_quantum_cost:.0f}")

Hybrid risk band summary
           count  fraud_rate
risk_band                   
Low           27       37.04
Medium       146       15.07
High          27       29.63
Hybrid quantum-classical cost: $1280
In [14]:
# Export the evaluation results and the selected best model for the dashboard.
prod = globals().get("production_results", None)
if prod and "evaluation_metrics" in prod:
    prod["evaluation_metrics"].to_json(
        "evaluation_results.json", orient="records", force_ascii=False
    )
    prod["evaluation_metrics"].to_csv("evaluation_results.csv", index=False)
if prod and "best_model" in prod:
    with open("best_model.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        fh.write(str(prod["best_model"]))

print(
    "Export complete: evaluation_results.json, evaluation_results.csv, and best_model.txt"
)
print(f"Best model: {prod['best_model'] if prod else 'N/A'}")

Export complete: evaluation_results.json, evaluation_results.csv, and best_model.txt
Best model: Quantum Neural Network (VQC)
